chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No. D-1554 of 2022 (Iftikhar Ali & 10 others v. P.O. Sindh & others) Present:- Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro & Mr. Justice Arbab Ali Hakro Mr. Abdul Qadir Khanzada, Advocate for the petitioners. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant A.G - Sindh. Date of Hearing & Order: 15-11-2023 O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J:- Petitioners being low-grade daily wages employees such as Pump Operators etc. in the Public Health Engineering Department, Naushahro Feroze, have filed this petition for regularization of their services on the ground that although they were appointed vide offer orders issued on 2nd October, 2022 for 90 days on daily wages. But since then their period of service has been continuously extended and they are still working. In proof, they have submitted bank statements showing credit of salaries in their accounts. It is also argued on their behalf that the said department has issued an advertisement dated 24.05.2021 inviting applications against the same posts and that process is still going on and no one so far has been appointed and that they have also applied for the same posts. 2. After arguing the matter at some length and in view of the various judgments of the Supreme Court and this Court that daily wages employees have no inalienable right of regularization, unless they go through due procedure of selection, the petitioners’ counsel has agreed to withdrawal of this petition subject to a condition that their applications would be considered sympathetically keeping in view the period they have already served in the department and in accordance with law. 3.

It is also argued on their behalf that the said department has issued an advertisement dated 24.05.2021 inviting applications against the same posts and that process is still going on and no one so far has been appointed and that they have also applied for the same posts. 2. After arguing the matter at some length and in view of the various judgments of the Supreme Court and this Court that daily wages employees have no inalienable right of regularization, unless they go through due procedure of selection, the petitioners’ counsel has agreed to withdrawal of this petition subject to a condition that their applications would be considered sympathetically keeping in view the period they have already served in the department and in accordance with law. 3. Learned Assistant A.G has no objection to it. Accordingly this petition is disposed of as not pressed, but strictly in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE 2 Cost. Petition No.D-1554 of 2022 Ahmad
